# WikiKit Documentation

A robots.txt-style index of the WikiKit documentation for LLMs. WikiKit is a
headless, AI-native knowledge system for humans and agents: Markdown-first
knowledge in, structured and agent-ready knowledge out. Sources are archived
verbatim, an LLM synthesizes maintained concept pages with verifiable claims
and citations, every change passes a human review gate as a ChangeProposal,
and the knowledge is reachable over HTTP/REST and MCP and exportable as
Markdown or OKF bundles. Each entry below has a title, path, and one-line
summary. For the complete documentation inlined into a single file, see
[docs/llms-full.txt](docs/llms-full.txt).

> Default local base URL: http://127.0.0.1:4060 â€” OpenAPI 3.1 spec at
> `/openapi.json`, MCP (Streamable HTTP) at `POST /mcp`. Authenticate with
> `Authorization: Bearer wk_...` or `X-API-Key: wk_...`; scopes are
> `knowledge:read`, `knowledge:propose`, `knowledge:review`, `knowledge:approve`, `admin` (approve implies review).

## Start here

- [Built-in agent guide](docs/agent-guide.md): WikiKit's code-versioned system knowledge â€” operating model, dynamic space routing, and capability-based no-CLI setup for MCP clients (live at `/agent-guide.md`).
- [README](README.md): What WikiKit is (the LLM-wiki loop, curation over collection), zero-config quickstart, standards-only integration, and links to everything else.
- [Coding-agent integration](docs/coding-agent-integration.md): Product-neutral lifecycle contract â€” compact startup grounding, task-dynamic additional spaces, and review-gated session learnings.
- [Example sources](examples/README.md): Two example source documents to ingest and the concept frontmatter reference for export/import bundles.
- [Concept frontmatter reference](examples/concept-frontmatter-reference.md): The lossless claims/relations frontmatter format used by `?format=md` bundles.

## API & usage

- Endpoints: `GET /v1/spaces`, `POST /v1/spaces`, `GET /v1/agent/briefing`, `POST /v1/agent/context`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/settings`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/ingest`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/ingest/document`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/agent/sessions`, `GET /v1/ingests/{id}`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/sources`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/sources/{id}`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/decisions`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/decisions/{slug}`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/concepts`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/concepts/{slug}`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/concepts/{slug}/history`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/search`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/query`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/proposals`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/proposals`, `GET /v1/proposals/{id}`, `POST /v1/proposals/{id}/approve`, `POST /v1/proposals/{id}/reject`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/lint`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/export`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/import`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/webhooks`, `POST /v1/spaces/{space}/webhooks`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/webhooks/{id}/deliveries`, `GET /v1/stats/mcp`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/stats/http`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/stats/usage`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/stats/reviews`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/stats/ingests`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/stats/knowledge`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/stats/llm`, `GET /v1/spaces/{space}/stats/webhooks`, `GET /v1/api-keys`, `POST /v1/api-keys`, `DELETE /v1/api-keys/{id}`, `GET /health`, `GET /ready`, `GET /metrics`, `GET /openapi.json`, `GET /review/{id}`, `GET /agent-guide.md`, `GET /llms.txt`, `GET /llms-full.txt`, `GET /.well-known/llms.txt`, `GET /.well-known/llms-full.txt`.
- Ingest is asynchronous: `202` + a `Location` header to poll; the result is a pending ChangeProposal. Approval is a separate, deliberate call.
- OpenAPI 3.1 is generated live from the route registry â€” fetch the spec at `/openapi.json`; a committed snapshot lives in [docs/openapi.json](docs/openapi.json).
- MCP (Streamable HTTP, outside the REST surface): tools `wikikit_guide`, `wikikit_spaces`, `wikikit_briefing`, `wikikit_context`, `wikikit_search`, `wikikit_read`, `wikikit_sources`, `wikikit_decisions`, `wikikit_history`, `wikikit_lint`, `wikikit_ingest`, `wikikit_ingest_status`, `wikikit_propose`, `wikikit_proposals`, `wikikit_review_proposal` â€” scope-gated visibility; `wikikit_context` is the zero-config task entry point when no hook supplied context; review requires `knowledge:review` (implied by `knowledge:approve`; the REST approve/reject endpoints still demand `knowledge:approve`), inspection of the full diff, and a native MCP form in which the human owns approve/reject plus the optional note. The review tool accepts only `proposal_id`; passing `decision`/`note` as arguments is refused with `approval_requires_human`. On a client without form elicitation the tool returns `outcome: "human_review_required"` with a `review_url` â€” the human opens that page (`GET /review/{id}`), authenticates with their own reviewer key and decides there; the agent polls `wikikit_proposals` and never approves through chat or REST for the human. Decline, cancel, timeout or invalid data likewise leaves the proposal pending.
- MCP self-description: `initialize` returns usage `instructions`; the tools-only-compatible `wikikit_guide` and resource `wikikit://system/agent-guide` expose immutable product guidance; `resources/list` + `resources/read` also serve this file (`wikikit://docs/llms.txt`) and the full docs (`wikikit://docs/llms-full.txt`) â€” no HTTP GET required.
- Remote MCP OAuth: `/mcp` exposes protected-resource discovery, dynamic client registration and PKCE. Human login can use a branded Firebase page, one or more OIDC providers, or a federated chooser. Interactive grants are limited by `WIKIKIT_OAUTH_ALLOWED_SCOPES` and per-provider policy; clients must request `knowledge:approve` explicitly.
- Interactive review clients: Codex should set `approval_policy = { granular = { mcp_elicitations = true } }` and `approvals_reviewer = "user"`; Claude Code requires 2.1.76+; ChatGPT support is conditional on the active connector advertising native form elicitation and passing a post-deploy canary. Without that capability the review hands off (`human_review_required` with a `review_url`): the human opens the review page and decides there as themselves, and the agent sees the result via `wikikit_proposals`.
- Usage analytics: opt in with `WIKIKIT_USAGE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED` plus `WIKIKIT_USAGE_HMAC_SECRET`. The ledger captures controlled HTTP/MCP/knowledge/review dimensions and exact product-local HMAC adoption onlyâ€”never content, prompts, queries, tool arguments/results, raw URLs, IP/UA, credentials or dynamic ids. Raw retention is `WIKIKIT_USAGE_RETENTION_DAYS`; aggregate readers support bounded UTC windows, traffic class and max-two groupings.

## Operating & deployment

- [Configuration](docs/CONFIGURATION.md): Every environment variable with its default â€” 12-factor, `WIKIKIT_*` prefix, production guards, zero-config dev defaults.
- [Deployment](docs/DEPLOYMENT.md): Single self-contained Bun binary, self-migrating boot, systemd, `/health` + `/ready` probes, graceful drain, release pipeline.
- [Security](SECURITY.md): Reporting vulnerabilities; operator hardening notes (key pepper, scoped keys, webhook signature verification, SSRF guard).
- [.env.example](.env.example): Annotated template of every configuration variable settable via `.env` (`NODE_ENV` is process-env only).

## Development

- [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): Request lifecycle, the `src/` module map, the proposal mechanics (staged rows + atomic SQL flip), ingest pipeline, MCP session model, and drift detection.
- [Interface contracts](docs/CONTRACTS.md): The binding contracts between subsystems â€” schema, routes, MCP tools, error envelope, state machines.
- [OKF v0.1 spec](docs/okf-v0.1.md): The vendored Open Knowledge Format specification the OKF export/import conforms to.
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): Dev setup (Bun + Docker), the `bun run gate` push gate, the five test tiers and which one a change needs, migration workflow, and conventions.
- [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Contributor Covenant v2.1.
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): Notable changes per release (Keep a Changelog / SemVer).
